footnotes.text: Foreordained: Jesus Christ was from all eternity the real Son of God according to His divine nature. When He became man, the glory of this nature was hidden away from men in the human nature which He had assumed.
Hab. 2, 4. The holiness and justice that God imparts to man, transforming him from a sinner into a son of God by adoption, and an heir to heaven.

God has given them up: as St. Thomas Aquinas says, not by impelling them to evil, but by deserting them. He justly withdrew His grace from them in punishment of their idolatry, and being thus abandoned by God, men followed the bent of fallen nature, and fell into the degradation of unnatural vice.
